pi_items.source: task.json line 18 specifies target_name. Task description line 5 says 'Name: "Preferred Language"'. Setup script line 18 searches for this exact name using 'q=Preferred+Language'. Export script line 24 queries for this name.

task.json line 19 specifies target_format. OpenMRS documentation (https://openmrs.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/docs/pages/25471074/Managing+Person+Attribute+Types) confirms java.lang.String is a standard format for Person Attribute Types. Task description line 5 explicitly states 'Format: "java.lang.String"'. Verifier line 68 checks for this exact format.

task.json line 20 specifies keyword 'language'. Task description line 5 provides full description: 'Patient\'s preferred language for translation and interpreter services'. The keyword 'language' is explicitly present in the full description. Verifier line 76 checks if the keyword appears in the description using case-insensitive matching.

task.json line 21 specifies target_searchable: true. Task description line 5 states 'Searchable: Checked/Enabled'. Setup script checks existing attribute's searchable status (line 20). Export script line 45 extracts the searchable property from OpenMRS API response. Verifier line 83 checks if searchable is strictly true (boolean).

task.json line 22 specifies admin_url. OpenMRS documentation (https://openmrs.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/docs/pages/25471074/Managing+Person+Attribute+Types) confirms Person Attribute Types are configured via /openmrs/admin path. Task_utils.sh line 9 shows OPENMRS_BASE_URL='https://localhost/openmrs', making admin_url 'https://localhost/openmrs/admin' the correct base admin interface URL. The full path to Person Attribute Types would be /openmrs/admin/person/personAttributeType.list.

privileged_info_summary: This task involves creating a synthetic Person Attribute Type in Bahmni OpenMRS. The target attribute is named 'Preferred Language' with Java String format, a description containing the keyword 'language', and searchable enabled. The OpenMRS admin interface is accessed at https://localhost/openmrs/admin. All metadata claims match the task specifications, OpenMRS documentation, and the verification scripts. The task is a UI interaction exercise where users navigate to the OpenMRS Administration interface and programmatically create this attribute type through the admin backend.
